data.title: RFK Jr. is linking Tylenol use in pregnancy to higher autism risk in a drop of Tylenol-maker KVUE stock. Report suggests folate treatment can reduce symptoms, emphasizing acetaminophen exposure and low folate for autism. HHS to publish this month.
data.description: The Health Secretary, Robert F. Kennedy Jr., is set to release a report revealing a potential link between the use of an over-the-counter pain reliever during pregnancy and a higher risk of autism. This report may impact Tylenol-maker KVUE as their stock is decreasing. Additionally, the report is expected to discuss research indicating that a folate-based treatment could potentially help reduce autism symptoms in specific individuals. The report, to be published by the Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) this month, will focus on two key factors associated with autism: low folate levels and prenatal exposure to acetaminophen (commonly known as Tylenol). For more information, consider visiting Benzinga.com.

data.title: Travis King appointed to head legal team at Texas LNG project in Houston; final investment decision expected by 2025.
data.description: Today, Glenfarne Group, LLC, the developer behind the Texas LNG project, announced the appointment of Travis King, a seasoned LNG legal professional based in Houston, to head the legal and compliance team for the project. Texas LNG, situated in the Port of Brownsville, Texas, is an upcoming liquefied natural gas export terminal operated by Glenfarne's subsidiary, Texas LNG Brownsville LLC. In his new role, King will be responsible for managing all legal and compliance activities for Texas LNG as the project nears its anticipated final investment decision slated for the conclusion of 2025.

data.title: Former skeptic Anthony Scaramucci became a Bitcoin believer after 8 years, attributing the shift to research and understanding.
data.description: Anthony Scaramucci, the CEO of SkyBridge Capital, revealed his journey from being a cryptocurrency skeptic to a supporter during a YouTube Q&A session. Initially, Scaramucci failed to grasp crypto due to his background in traditional finance. However, diligent research enabled him to shift his perspective. It took him eight years to overcome his skepticism and make his first Bitcoin investment. This transformation showcases the evolving landscape of digital assets. For more details, refer to the story available on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Asante Gold Corp receives C$237M from equity offering, completes Kinross restructuring, secures $500M financing.
data.description: Vancouver, BC, Aug. 12, 2025 - Asante Gold Corporation (CSE: ASE | GSE: ASG | FRANKFURT:1A9 | OTCQX: ASGOF) ("Asante" or "the Company") has successfully met the requirements for the initial drawdown under the definitive agreements tied to a $500 million financing package. This achievement marks the fulfillment of escrow release conditions linked to a bought deal private placement involving 163,300,000 subscription receipts of the Company priced at C$1.45 each, generating total proceeds of about C$237 million ($M). The private placement, completed on July 7, 2025, has propelled the Company towards its financing goals. All financial figures are stated in U.S. dollars unless specified otherwise.

data.title: CleanCore Solutions closed a $175,000,420 private placement, launching the Dogecoin Treasury and House of Doge.
data.description: The Dogecoin Foundation has announced the establishment of the official Dogecoin Treasury, alongside its new corporate entity, the House of Doge. This move signifies a significant milestone for Dogecoin, with the Treasury set to manage a substantial amount of Dogecoin funds, currently estimated to be at [insert number]. The House of Doge will serve as the Foundation's commercial arm, focusing on expanding Dogecoin's presence in the market and exploring strategic business opportunities. This development is expected to further enhance Dogecoin's standing and influence within the cryptocurrency community.

data.title: Leica Gallery Boston showcases "Greetings from Niagara" exhibition by Natalia Neuhaus, running from Sept 19.
data.description: Exploring the intersection of photography, history, and science, an intriguing exploration of Niagara Falls will be showcased by Leica Gallery in Boston. The exhibit, titled Greetings from Niagara and captured by renowned photographer Natalia Neuhaus, will be displayed from September 19 onward. This unique display promises to offer a fresh perspective on the iconic falls, blending artistry with historical context and scientific insight.

data.title: Avenue Living achieves over $8.5 billion AUM, records $1.2 billion acquisitions in FY, sees $600 million annual run rate.
data.description: Avenue Living, a leading private real estate investment firm in North America, is experiencing record growth and momentum, with 1,000+ employees across seven provinces and 20 states. Their assets under management have surpassed $8.5 billion, with over $1.2 billion in acquisitions completed so far. The firm generates nearly $600 million in annual recurring revenue. In May 2025, they received an investment-grade debt rating and issued $250 million in bonds. Avenue Living also launched a global expansion offering of $350 million, oversubscribed due to strong investor demand. Their disciplined growth strategy, focusing on workforce housing, self-storage, and farmland, has led to significant expansion and strong financial performance, with over $450 million in net fund flows year-to-date.

data.title: Inflation expected to stay above 2% target through year-end, economists predict.
data.description: Most economists predict that inflation will stay significantly higher than the central bank's 2 percent target for the rest of the year.

data.title: EU fines Google $3.5B for violating antitrust laws, accused of stifling competition in online advertising.
data.description: EU authorities have accused a major American technology company of leveraging its market size and power to undermine competitors in the online advertising sector. This action has the potential to provoke anger from the Trump administration.
